description_long: This object is a vintage push-button landline telephone, featuring a rectangular base with a sloped front panel and a classic corded handset resting on top. The base is made of off-white plastic with a slightly yellowed, aged appearance, and it has a set of large, dark brown buttons with white numerals and symbols, arranged in a grid for dialing. To the right of the keypad, there are several additional rectangular buttons, likely for functions such as redial, hold, or speakerphone. The coiled cord connects the handset to the left side of the base, and the handset itself is ergonomically shaped to fit comfortably in the hand. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, and the object shows some signs of wear and surface discoloration. This type of telephone would typically be found on a desk or countertop in an office, home, or public building.
description: A vintage corded push-button telephone with a rectangular off-white plastic base, large brown keypad, and a matching handset connected by a coiled cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handset resting on the base, the full keypad, and function buttons clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the sloped profile of the base and the coiled cord emerging from the handset.
description_view_2: Rear view with the back of the handset and the keypad partially visible, showing the telephone's depth.
description_view_3: Right side view displaying the side profile of the base and the cord trailing away from the handset.
